channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Programming Competition on April 4th
item.description: Date: Saturday, April 4, 2026 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM - OPT-RPI, RPI’s yearly programming competition, is this weekend on April 4th. The competition will be in Lally 102 at 12:00 PM. Join for food, fun, and $2000 in prizes!
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 4/5-4/11
item.description: Week of 4/5- 4/11: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! -Strengths: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center (Beta Classroom) -MAU: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Easter Holiday Notice: Sunday, April 5th
item.description: No drop-in tutoring during the Easter holiday at the Success Center. Small group & one-to-one sessions may still be available to book.
item.author: Students

item.title: Open Data Hackathon - Build with AI - Hudson Valley, NY
item.description: Date: Saturday, April 4, 2026 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM - Come and join us for the Open Data Hackathon where you will sharpen your tech talent on real world problems that affect our region. It’ll be a fun day of problem-solving innovation and collaboration that also looks great on your resume! More detail available in the link below: https://gdg.community.dev/events/details/google-gdg-hudson-presents-open-data-hackathon-build-with-ai-hudson-valley-ny-1/cohost-gdg-hudson/
item.author: Students

item.title: Professor Saggi Nevo Appointed Director of New Center in Lally
item.description: Lally is thrilled to announce the appointment of Professor Saggi Nevo as the new Director of the Center for Management for Ethics in Emerging Technologies, effective March 1, 2026. An Associate Professor of Business Analytics and Information Systems in Lally, Professor Nevo steps into this leadership role to advance scholarship, teaching, and thought leadership at the intersection of ethics and emerging technologies, an area of growing significance in alignment with Lally’s strategic priorities.
item.author: Community

item.title: Professor Raffi Garcia appointed Director of the Center for Financial Studies
item.description: Lally is thrilled to announce the appointment of Professor Raffi Garcia as the new Director of the Center for Financial Studies, effective March 1, 2026. An Associate Professor of Finance in Lally, Professor Garcia steps into this leadership role to advance research, teaching, and engagement in the field of finance and emerging technologies, and to strengthen Lally’s impact in this critical area.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Students Experiment with Music Performance for April 9 Concert
item.description: RPI students will experiment with music performance in “Constellations” at EMPAC on April 9. The performance explores structured improvisation, and will be led by sound artist Nick Dunston. Also featured will be a generative algorithm in place of a traditional score, as well as an appearance by DJ Rekha. Learn more: https://bit.ly/4c1gLEW
item.author: Community

item.title: Writing for Fellowships & Grants: Workshop for PhD Students
item.description: Learn approaches to presenting content & editing principles to develop concise effective applications. Previous participants have secured major awards, including international fellowships! April 8th, 2:00-4:00 PM, In person. Register to attend! https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Questions? madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Spring 2026 End of Term Course Surveys in HelioCampus: Adding Additional Questions to a Course Section (Faculty/Instructor Training)
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - This webinar offers faculty/instructors of course sections to add additional questions to their student end of term course evaluations. This is an optional process and only needed if instructors wanted to add other questions than what is on the institute course evaluation. Please click the link in the See section to register for the session.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Getting Started with Percipio: Navigation & Q&A”
item.description: This brief course is designed to help you get comfortable using Percipio, RPI’s digital learning platform. You’ll learn how to navigate the main dashboard, search for content, access assigned learning, and track your progress. We’ll also walk through common questions users have and show you where to find help when needed. Whether you’re brand new or just need a refresher, this session will ensure you’re ready to make the most of Percipio.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees—working collaboratively to align expectations, support development, and monitor progress toward achieving goals. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:30 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Commencement Volunteer Opportunities
item.description: Commencement is one of the best opportunities at RPI to connect with fellow faculty, staff, and our graduating students! To ensure a memorable experience for our graduating class, we need assistance from everyone. Please consider volunteering at commencement or finding other ways to support the event leading up to the big day. Please click the title to access the online form and confirm your participation with a manager beforehand.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty Quick Learn Webinar: Tips for Planning Your Condensed Course
item.description:
Date: Monday, April 13, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - The Planning Your Condensed Course webinar is meant to assist instructors teaching in The Arch or short semester courses in planning and presenting their course material in innovative, effective, and carefully considered ways. In this webinar we will offer quick tips on best practices in condensing coursework. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
